summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the area of a parallelogram with a base of 10 cm and a height of 6 cm?

Area of a Parallelogram A = bh where b is the base and h is the height (perpendicular to the base). Area of a Triangle A = ½bh where b is the base and h is the corresponding height. The other options — '30 cm²', '80 cm²', and '16 cm²' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the area of a triangle with a base of 14 cm and a height of 9 cm?

The area of a triangle is A = ½ × base × height. Substitute the given values and calculate. The other options — '126 cm²', '56 cm²', and '72 cm²' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

A parallelogram has a base of 15 m and an area of 120 m². What is its height?

Area of a Parallelogram A = bh where b is the base and h is the height (perpendicular to the base). Area of a Triangle A = ½bh where b is the base and h is the corresponding height. The other options — '10 m', '6 m', and '12 m' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
